label: Heavy going
summary:
A grim Monday on two fronts. The April Iran–Israel truce broke down: Israel struck military sites in Tehran, Isfahan and Tabriz, Iran fired missiles toward Israel in the first direct exchange since the spring ceasefire, and Israeli strikes on Lebanon continued. By day's end Tehran announced it was halting its attacks but warned of 'much more severe and crushing' action if strikes on southern Lebanon persist; Trump said both sides want 'an immediate ceasefire' while cautioning that 'stupidity' could derail it. Oil jumped roughly 4.5% to above $94 a barrel on renewed Strait of Hormuz fears, though equity futures held up and the S&P 500 sat near record territory.

Asia absorbed a separate blow: a magnitude 7.8 earthquake — the Philippines' strongest since 1990 — struck off Mindanao, killing at least 15 (some counts to 32), injuring more than 200, collapsing buildings in General Santos and triggering brief tsunami warnings across the western Pacific; President Marcos shut schools for 3.2 million students. Elsewhere the mood frayed: US Defense Secretary Hegseth drew historians' condemnation for tying D-Day remembrance to an anti-immigration 'invasion' message, and South Africa moved to crack down on migration amid anti-foreigner unrest. Against the gloom there was real progress — Nigeria's military freed 360 people held by Boko Haram, a pancreatic-cancer pill doubled survival in trials, and Europe set a record by removing 603 river barriers in a single year.

drivers.headline: April truce collapses: Israel strikes Tehran, Isfahan and Tabriz as Iran fires missiles in first direct exchange since spring
Iran says it is halting attacks on Israel but threatens 'much more severe' action if strikes on southern Lebanon continue
Magnitude 7.8 quake off Mindanao — Philippines' strongest since 1990 — kills at least 15, injures 200+, triggers tsunami warnings
Quake death toll rises toward 32 as buildings collapse in General Santos; Marcos cancels classes for 3.2 million students
Oil surges ~4.5% to above $94 on renewed Strait of Hormuz fears, though S&P 500 futures hold near record levels
Hegseth uses D-Day anniversary speech to warn of migrant 'invasion'; historians call it 'grotesque stupidity'
South Africa's Ramaphosa announces migration crackdown amid anti-foreigner marches, insists 'no space for xenophobia'
Russian drone strikes nuclear-waste storage facility near Chornobyl as Moscow intensifies attacks on Ukrainian infrastructure
Nigeria's military rescues 360 people held captive by Boko Haram in the Mandara Mountains
Pancreatic cancer pill daraxonrasib doubles median survival from 6.7 to 13.2 months in 500-person trial
Europe removes record 603 river barriers in 2025, reconnecting 2,300 miles of waterways for migrating fish
Wind and solar generated more of the world's electricity than gas for the first time in April (22% vs 20%)

sources_consulted: Wikipedia (Current events / Mindanao earthquake / Iran war ceasefire)
